detect: settings.gradle(.kts) at repo root OR in android/, AND (iosApp/ dir OR a *.xcodeproj)
gatesComment: CORRECTED 2026-07-19. This previously read that Roborazzi here is record-by-default and that no test command avoids snapshot writes. That was true only because android/gradle.properties set roborazzi.test.record=true globally (added 2026-06-12, "we just want the pictures"). The line is GONE. Each task now means what its name says: testDebugUnitTest runs tests and writes NO baselines; recordRoborazziDebug regenerates them; verifyRoborazziDebug compares and FAILS on a diff. Verified 2026-07-19 by forced --rerun-tasks in both directions: a full test run rewrote 0 PNGs, and verify reported 103 unchanged / 0 recorded. This restores the snapshot gate's meaning — under the old flag the fast gate re-recorded goldens from current code BEFORE the snapshot gate compared them, so a genuine UI regression could not fail it. Some baselines (e.g. feature/squadlist SquadListScreenSnapshotTest.squadList_*) still render nondeterministically across machines; treat those as environmental noise unless a source change explains them.

gateNotes.snapshot: Baselines are now VERIFIED, not rewritten (see gatesComment). A regression here is a REAL 'fail' — do not re-record to make it pass. If a NEW component has no baseline, run `JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@17 ./gradlew recordRoborazziDebug`, then report 'pass' with a detail NAMING every recorded file so a human can eyeball it. If an EXISTING baseline changed, that is a 'fail' unless the change is intended AND you say plainly which surface changed and why; re-record deliberately in that case, never reflexively. Popup-layer surfaces (DropdownMenu, ModalBottomSheet, AlertDialog, coach marks) are UNREACHABLE by captureRoboImage — extract the content into a composable and snapshot THAT directly, the way DiceTrayContent and EndRoundConfirmContent are. Flag any new baseline that is blank, near-blank, or byte-identical to another: identical siblings mean a fixture that does not vary, and blanks mean the composable rendered nothing.
gateNotes.e2e: First run `adb devices`; if a device/emulator is attached, run the e2e gate. If none is attached, report status 'skipped' with detail 'no emulator running'.

conventions: Kotlin Multiplatform (Android + iOS) app with Compose Multiplatform UI. Shared logic in commonMain; platform code in androidMain/iosMain. Modules: app/, core/*, feature/*, build-logic/ (convention plugins). Follow the existing DI, app-shell, and navigation patterns already in the codebase — read neighboring files before adding anything. testTag strings live in a shared TestTags file in commonMain; iOS mirrors them by hand in the iosApp UI tests. Match surrounding naming, comment density, and idiom. No new dependencies without strong justification.

reviewDimensions.ask: Logic bugs, edge cases, null/empty/error handling, state that fails to reset between operations.
commonMain/platform split is correct, DI wiring matches the codebase, module boundaries respected, no dead code, no needless new dependencies.
Uses the project's design-system components, correct testTags for anything user-facing, snapshot-worthy states are covered by Roborazzi.

procedures.snapshot: Roborazzi tasks are the `Debug`-suffixed variants (verifyRoborazziDebug / recordRoborazziDebug). New composables need a baseline recorded first.
procedures.ios: This factory only runs the Android gates. Build the iOS framework and run the iOS UI tests separately (xcodebuild) before any release.
